instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `check-not-zero` that evaluates an expression once; if the value is zero, signal an error; otherwise return the value.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (DENOMINATOR X)))
            (WHEN (ZEROP #:G1) (ERROR "Value must not be zero"))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (DENOMINATOR X)))
            (WHEN (ZEROP #:G112) (ERROR "Value must not be zero"))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
